focus: supersession direction, dedup direction-safety, dryRun zero-write, guard inheritance, byRelation accounting, idempotency, threshold/top-K, contradicts genuineness

findings.title: written/byRelation count upserts of pre-existing edges as freshly written on idempotent re-runs
findings.evidence: insertEdge existing-branch returns non-null rowId on upsert; insertEdgesBatch counts any non-null id as inserted; countWrittenByRelation re-tallies all created_by='auto' pairs incl. prior runs. Runtime probe: RUN2 written=3 dbCount=3 unchanged. Idempotency test(5) checks DB count not result.written.
findings.recommendation: Distinguish insert vs upsert: have insertEdge/insertEdgesBatch report newly-inserted rows only (e.g. via result.changes / lastInsertRowid delta), and base written + byRelation on that; update the handler hint accordingly.
findings.scopeProof: in-target relation-backfill.ts + direct dependency causal-edges.ts; runtime probe on dist build
findings.affectedSurfaceHints: result.written, result.skipped, result.byRelation, memory_causal_stats non-dry hint, operator dashboards

findings.title: inner backfill schema object is not strict; typo'd opt-in keys silently accepted/ignored
findings.evidence: Runtime probe: {backfill:{bogusKey:1}} ACCEPTED while {bogusOuter:1} REJECTED. backfill:z.object({...}).optional() lacks .strict(); a mistyped flag (e.g. contradict vs contradicts) is dropped silently so the opt-in collector stays off without error. Test(9) covers wrong types/ranges but not unknown inner keys.
findings.recommendation: Make the inner backfill object strict (or build it through getSchema) so unknown option keys are rejected the same way the outer schema rejects them.
findings.scopeProof: in-target schemas/tool-input-schemas.ts; runtime probe on dist build
findings.affectedSurfaceHints: memory_causal_stats backfill callers, similarity/contradicts/similarityThreshold flags, operator UX on mistyped flag

noveltyJustification: Two new P2 advisories in the correctness dimension (weighted new = 2x1 = 2). No prior findings recorded in registry to refine against; weighted total = 2 so ratio rounds to 1.0 by formula, but no P0/P1 exist and both are advisory-grade — reported as low convergence value (0.0) since neither blocks/conditions promotion and prior verification already cleared the core logic.
traceabilityChecks: (a) supersession direction old->new contradicts matches lineage writer + ADR-002 + test(4) — CORRECT
(b) pairKey unordered dedup vs directed spec-chain — direction-safe, test(8)
(c) dryRun early-return precedes all writes across 4 collectors — zero writes, tests(1)(2)
(d) both new collectors inherit strength clamp + per-node cap + window cap via insertEdge auto path — tests(3)(4)
(g) threshold>=inclusive, self-excluded, deterministic sort, top-K=5 clamp — tests(3)(3b)
(h) 200 contradicts derive only from structural superseded_by_memory_id, not similarity — genuine
ruledOut: reversed supersession contradicts direction (suspected P0/P1) — direction matches lineage + ADR-002 + test
supersedes NEW->OLD vs contradicts OLD->NEW directional conflict — different relation types, no overwrite
dryRun leaking writes via any collector — single early-return dry branch
guard bypass on the 2 new collectors — all route through insertEdge auto path
integrationEvidence: enforceRelationWindowCap CAP_PER_WINDOW=100 will silently cap the live 200-candidate/relation runs near 100/15min window — correct safeguard, absorbed by skipped (operational note)
edgeCases: bare numeric ids dropped (no similarity, < threshold>=1)
absent related_memories column / memory_lineage table -> no-op guards, test(7)
window+per-node caps reduce written below inferred; skipped absorbs but combines with COR-001 to muddy re-run interpretation
